title: Percent-encoded valid address test never encodes the address
reasoning: The test claims to verify that a percent-encoded valid address is decoded before validation, but `VALID_ADDR` contains only unreserved URL characters (`0`, `x`, and hex letters), so `encodeURIComponent(VALID_ADDR)` returns the exact same string. This means the regression test would still pass if the valid-address decode path were removed or broken; only the malformed-encoding catch path is currently exercised.
reproduction: In a JS REPL, `encodeURIComponent('0x' + 'a'.repeat(40)) === '0x' + 'a'.repeat(40)` evaluates to true.
recommendation: Use an actually encoded-but-valid address parameter, for example `0%78${'a'.repeat(40)}` or another fixture where at least one character is percent-encoded and decoding is required to pass `isValidAddress`.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The existing test name and comments describe the intended behavior, but the fixture is unchanged by `encodeURIComponent`, so it only covers the same no-op path as a plain address.
suggestedRegressionTest: Change the encoded fixture to a string such as `0%78${'a'.repeat(40)}` and assert the client receives `0x${'a'.repeat(40)}`.
minimumFixScope: Update the single server-shell test fixture in `ui-dashboard/src/app/address-book/[address]/__tests__/page.server.test.ts`.

history.note: Fixed by using an actually percent-encoded valid address fixture (0%78...) so the server-shell test requires decoding.
